paper_title: Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning
core_innovation: Converting binary or scalar feedback from the environment into verbal feedback in the form of a textual summary

methodology.feedback_mechanism: Verbal reinforcement learning that adds self-reflective feedback as additional context for the LLM agent
feedback_generation: Pre-defined heuristics for common failure cases
Self-evaluation (binary classification using LLMs or self-written unit tests)
methodology.learning_process: Self-reflective feedback acts as a 'semantic' gradient signal providing concrete directions for improvement

advantages: Lightweight and doesn't require finetuning the LLM
Allows for more nuanced forms of feedback
Provides explicit and interpretable episodic memory
Offers explicit hints for actions in future episodes
disadvantages: Relies on the power of the LLM's self-evaluation capabilities

results.decision_making: 22% improvement over strong baseline approaches in 12 iterative learning steps

title: Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning
